name: Honey-Buttermilk Whole-Wheat Rolls
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Unsalted butter or margarine, melted
recipeInstructions:
These rolls have a soft texture and delicate flavor but a firm crust. They look very inviting, deeply browned with a dusting of flour. They make great moderately sized sandwiches.
Basic Sponge:
In a food processor fitted with a metal blade, mix together the buttermilk, salt, honey, and melted butter until smooth. Add the flours one-quarter cup at a time, pulsing several times to incorporate each addition. Process the dough until it is supple, elastic, and slightly sticky, adding more water or flour by teaspoon if needed. The dough should be very sticky, almost wet.
Transfer the dough to a large plastic bag, squeezing out all air and sealing tightly at the top for room for expansion. Set the bag in a bowl and let it rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
Divide the dough into half. Cut each in half again, then quarter into 4 equal pieces, weighing about 2 1/2 ounces each. Shape each piece into a round roll by pinching the bottom of the dough. Place them 2 inches apart on an oiled baking sheet, with the pinched side down. Sprinkle with flour and cover loosely with plastic wrap. Let the rolls rise in a warm place until almost doubled, about 1 hour.
About 15 minutes before baking, preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Bake the rolls until they are deeply browned and sound hollow when tapped on the bottom, about 20 minutes. Remove from the pan and transfer to a wire rack. Makes 16 rolls.
Each roll contains approximately:
162 calories; 231 mg sodium; 4 mg cholesterol; 3 grams fat; 31 grams carbohydrates; 5 grams protein; 0.4 grams fiber; 16% of calories from fat.
